rulings.Case Title: CHARLES W. MOORE VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 8, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Puget Sound Commerce Center, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a), 1010.6, 12a and 12c. Plaintiff's objection based on timeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for summary judgment will be continued pursuant to plaintiffs' C.C.P. § 437c(h) request, which is granted. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD KEITH ANDERS ET AL VS. AMERICAN BILTRITE INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re: Duty Of Shipyard Owner
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 8, 2015 in Department 503, Line 8. Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication re: duty of shipyard owner is denied. The Court cannot summarily adjudicate the issue of whether a non-defendant did or did not owe a duty to a plaintiff. (C.C.P. § 437c(f)(1).) However, even if the Court could, the motion would be denied because plaintiff failed to sustain his initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff failed to sustain his burden of demonstrating that defendants do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the shipyard owner did owe a duty to plaintiff. Plaintiff failed to sustain his burden of submitting affirmative evidence to demonstrate that the shipyard owner did not owe him a duty and thus cannot sustain his burden under the negation of an element prong. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
